question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the prune(endTimestamp) call on RecordFileRepository to delete the record_file entry that the importer's RecordStreamFileListener uses as its last-processed file marker, causing the importer to re-download and re-process already-confirmed transactions and creating a confirmation loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method reads retentionProperties at runtime (not at startup) to cause a time-of-check-time-of-use race where the retention period is changed between the findLatestWithOffset() call and the actual prune() execution, deleting more data than intended?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the batchPeriod to be set to 1 nanosecond (minimum Duration), resulting in hasNext() calling findNextBetween() once per nanosecond of historical data, generating millions of database queries that exhaust the connection pool and block new transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum execution time limit on the prune() method to cause it to run for hours on a large dataset, holding the synchronized lock and preventing any other synchronized importer operations from executing, halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() to execute a DELETE on the transaction table that acquires an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, blocking concurrent SELECT queries from the REST API and causing clients to perceive a total network shutdown even if the importer is still running?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatestWithOffset() uses a subquery (select max(consensus_end) from record_file) that is evaluated at query time, allowing a concurrent transaction insertion to shift the max value between the subquery evaluation and the outer query, causing an incorrect pruning boundary?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the RecordFileIterator to enter a state where current is non-null but not equal to max (by reference), and findNextBetween() always returns the same record file, creating an infinite loop that permanently blocks the importer scheduler thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method does not validate that the pruning boundary (maxTimestamp) is strictly less than the current network time, allowing it to prune data that has not yet been fully confirmed by the network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the forEach loop over retentionRepositories to process repositories in an order where transaction data is deleted before record_file data, leaving orphaned record_file entries that cause the importer to attempt re-processing of already-deleted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method's catch block only logs errors without triggering any circuit breaker or health check failure, allowing the importer to appear healthy while silently failing to prune, eventually causing disk exhaustion that halts transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the record_file table to have only one entry, triggering the special case in hasNext() where findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd()) returns that single entry, and then prune() deletes it, leaving the importer with no record file anchor and halting stream processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Scheduled annotation uses fixedDelay (not fixedRate), meaning if prune() runs for longer than the frequency period, the next execution starts immediately after completion, creating a continuous pruning loop that monopolizes database resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a PostgreSQL deadlock between the prune() transaction (deleting from transaction table) and the importer's parser transaction (inserting into transaction table), causing both to roll back and the importer to enter an infinite retry loop that halts new transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check ensuring that the pruning boundary is not within the active processing window of the importer, causing prune() to delete transactions that the importer's balance reconciliation job is currently reading, corrupting reconciliation state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the endTimestamp = current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod calculation to produce Long.MAX_VALUE or Long.MIN_VALUE through overflow, causing findNextBetween() to receive invalid parameters and return unexpected results that corrupt the iterator state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method does not check whether the importer is currently actively processing a stream file before starting deletion, causing a race condition where active transaction data is deleted mid-parse?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the database to run out of temporary space during the large DELETE operation inside transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult(), causing the database to crash and halt all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method uses retentionProperties.getPeriod() which is a mutable @Data class, allowing a concurrent thread to modify the period between the findLatestWithOffset() call and the iterator construction, causing an inconsistent pruning boundary?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the record_file table to be populated with entries having identical consensusEnd values, causing findNextBetween() to return the same record file repeatedly and creating an infinite loop in the RecordFileIterator?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method does not acquire any distributed lock before executing, allowing two importer instances in a high-availability setup to simultaneously prune overlapping timestamp ranges and cause database constraint violations that halt both importers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the prune(endTimestamp) call to delete transaction_signature rows that are still needed by the importer's signature verification process, causing the importer to fail signature checks on new stream files and halt ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method's log.warn() message when latest.isEmpty() does not trigger any alerting or health check failure, allowing the importer to silently skip pruning indefinitely while disk fills up and eventually halts transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Stopwatch instance created in RecordFileIterator to overflow its internal counter during an extremely long-running prune operation, causing the elapsed time calculation to produce a negative value and corrupting the rate metric used for monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method does not validate that retentionRepositories is non-empty before iterating, allowing a misconfiguration where no repositories are pruned but the job still marks itself as having run, preventing future pruning and causing disk exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() to fail with a TransactionSystemException that is not caught by the inner lambda but propagates to the outer try-catch, leaving the database in a partially pruned state that the importer cannot recover from?
